🔥 Hot Prompt Patterns This Week
•Camera: “Single continuous FPV drone take”, “sweeping lateral tracking with parallax”, “low-to-aerial transition”
•Motion: Heavy physics (feathers rippling, debris into lens, hair wind), sound design (no music, SFX only)
•Consistency: Strict face/dress references + negative prompts for distortion
•Tools: Kling 3.0 still dominates realism & motion; Seedance 2.0 for longer continuity

prompt:
“You are a no-BS growth hacker for creators selling digital products.
Generate a complete short AI video concept based on this style: [paste video description or idea here, e.g. “three pigeons on a rooftop with a big red button”].

Output in bullets only:

1.  Exact 45–60 second story beat sheet (setup, conflict, twist, punchline)

2.  Detailed Runway / Kling / Luma prompts for each scene (camera angles, character expressions, voice direction)

3.  5 viral X/LinkedIn hooks that drive views to a $29–$97 digital product

4.  One simple product idea (e.g. “AI Storytelling Template Pack” or “10 Ready-to-Gen Short Scripts”) with pricing and sales angle

5.  Quick edit checklist to fix common AI glitches (mouth sync, consistency) Focus on high-engagement comedy or relatable creator pain. Zero fluff.”

One creator I shared a similar version with turned a dumb “office chair rebellion” idea into a $1.2k digital product launch in a weekend.

prompt:
“In a 3x3 grid, show this character in different angles, keep the scene the same, random poses.

This is far simpler and more efficient than my old prompts. You can generate multiple times and just pick the keyframes you like most.

2. Extract a high-res keyframe (Super stable trick)
Take a screenshot of the keyframe you want from the 3×3 grid, send it back to Nano Banana Pro, and simply say:

“Give me a high-resolution version.”
This method is much more stable than relying on complex upscaling prompts.

3. Generate the video with Kling 2.5 Turbo
Upload the first and last frames to Kling 2.5 Turbo and use this prompt:

“The camera very slowly and smoothly lowers on a boom.”

From my testing, Kling 2.5 Turbo offers the best balance of stability and cost — other models are either less consistent or noticeably more expensive.

4. Final speed adjustment with @ReflctWillie’s tool (Critical step)

Use the tool built by @ReflctWillie to fine-tune the playback speed of each clip.

This step is essential for getting that premium cinematic feel.

I’ll drop the tool link in the comments.

title: Famous Dish Toy Package
scenario: a verified Kling X prompt focused on <instruction> do this for ANY famous dish Input, preserving the original source language, camera intent, motion cues, and visible generation instructions
description: The source X post explicitly mentions Kling and includes visible prompt text in the original post body. Metrics are captured from the X tweet snapshot.
action: Use the original Kling prompt for <instruction> do this for ANY famous dish Input as a video-generation structure with your own subject, setting, camera path, and duration constraints.
prompt:
<instruction> do this for ANY famous dish
Input A is a famous dish (photo or dish name). Analyze origin, key ingredients, signature tools, and the most iconic cooking moment.
Optional Input B is a style reference (collectible toy packaging / culinary museum). If missing, choose: high-end figurine packaging meets artisan food styling.
Goal: A clear acrylic display box containing a miniature kitchen scene of the dish being made, surrounded by ingredient compartments like a premium kit.
Rules:

Center: miniature cook mid-action performing the defining technique (flip, stir, torch, knead, ladle, carve)

Surrounding compartments: neatly organized micro-ingredients and micro-garnishes (each with tiny labels)

Include mini tools unique to the dish (specific pan, grill, mortar, steamer, tongs, whisk, etc.)

Include a small origin map tile and a date/era reference (tasteful, not over-precise if uncertain)

Packaging color accents derived from the cuisine’s cultural palette (pottery, textiles, flags—subtle)

Photoreal acrylic reflections, soft shadows, impeccable cleanliness
Output: ONE image, portrait-oriented artisan product photography, crisp and premium

</instruction>

title: Skyway Character Video Workflow
scenario: a verified Kling X prompt focused on Get the Kling video prompt farther below. Use, preserving the original source language, camera intent, motion cues, and visible generation instructions
description: The source X post explicitly mentions Kling and includes visible prompt text in the original post body. Metrics are captured from the X tweet snapshot.
action: Use the original Kling prompt for Get the Kling video prompt farther below. Use as a video-generation structure with your own subject, setting, camera path, and duration constraints.
prompt:
Get the Kling video prompt farther below. Use it as you like.

I combined a photo I took in the Minneapolis skyway, (Macy’s building) which is the background for the scene, with an image I created using Higgsfield SOUL. I used Seedream for the edit.

Then I created a detailed Kling 3 video prompt for the sequence you see here. The prompt is farther below.

If you’re not using AI to vibe code video prompts, and targeting them towards specific platforms, give it a try.

AI can write an incredible prompt, and it can also tailor it towards your target platform.

Each platform has different requirements, and AI has detailed knowledge of what to do. ChatGPT, Google Gemini, Grok, Claude …. Whatever your favorite is will work.

I used Seedream to take the image from Higgsfield SOUL (see my previous post for image and image prompt for her) and combine them into one image, and add Black Doc Marten boots.

I gave ChatGPT my starting frame for the video, and had it create a detailed Kling prompt for the video for the storyline I had in my mind.

🔥 AI Workflow:
AI image creation → Higgsfield SOUL
AI image edit → Seedream
Image touch up → Photoshop
Video prompt creation → ChatGPT
Video creation → Kling 3.0

🔥 Kling video prompt:
Use the uploaded image as the exact starting frame and exact character identity. Maintain full consistency in face, hair, outfit, proportions, lighting, and environment. Do not alter wardrobe, background architecture, or color tones.

Scene:
Modern industrial interior with high ceiling and large rectangular overhead light. Clean, minimal, slightly cool color grading. Quiet open-space atmosphere.

Camera:
Static camera, eye-level. Medium-to-tight framing that allows subject to walk into a close-up. No camera movement. Shallow depth of field, subject always in sharp focus.

Action:
The woman begins standing at a distance, centered in frame.

She walks forward slowly and naturally toward the camera with steady, grounded steps. Her boots produce soft, realistic footstep sounds.

As she approaches, her gaze locks onto the lens. Her expression is calm, slightly curious, subtly challenging.
She stops about 2 feet from the camera. Final framing shows her full face, neck, and top of shirt clearly.

At the end:
She leans slightly closer and peers directly into the lens.
Her head tilts subtly side-to-side in small, controlled movements, as if trying to see the viewer through the camera.

Her expression shifts to a faint, thoughtful curiosity.

Performance Details:
Natural body movement, no jitter or unnatural motion.
Eye tracking remains locked to the lens once she begins walking.

Micro-expressions are subtle and realistic.

Audio:
Minimal sound design.
▪️ Light ambient air tone (open interior space)
▪️ Soft, grounded boot footsteps

Timing:
6–8 seconds total duration.

Style:
Photorealistic, cinematic, natural motion. No stylization, no distortion, no added effects.

title: Outfit Replacement Workflow
scenario: a verified Kling X prompt focused on Replace the person’s outfit perfectly with [dress/character name], preserving the original source language, camera intent, motion cues, and visible generation instructions
description: The source X post explicitly mentions Kling and includes visible prompt text in the original post body. Metrics are captured from the X tweet snapshot.
action: Use the original Kling prompt for Replace the person’s outfit perfectly with [dress/character name] as a video-generation structure with your own subject, setting, camera path, and duration constraints.
prompt:
Replace the person’s outfit perfectly with [dress/character name], without changing anything else. Do not resize, move, or alter anything else.

Set the resolution to 1k or 2k. Avoid 4k, as it tends to reveal artifacts.

Step 3.
➡Creating animations with Kling Motion Control in @higgsfield_ai
➡ Upload your original video and the image generated by NanoBanan Pro. Important! Don't upload the entire video file—that gets expensive. However, don't make the clip too short either; you need enough buffer for the action to play out so it’s easy to stitch together in post-production.
➡ Use this prompt for the animation:
Transfer the motion from the attached video to the attached image perfectly.
Then hit Create.

You can also describe various background effects and additional actions in the prompt.

Step 4.
Advanced shots: Background replacement
➡ Choose your desired background from Pinterest or another platform.
➡ Ask Gemini to analyze the background and create a detailed prompt for NanoBanan Pro. For example:
Create a prompt to place my photo into this background perfectly, matching lighting and perspective.
➡ Paste the resulting prompt into NanoBanan Pro along with an image of your face and click "Create." You will get an image of yourself on the new background.
➡ Return to Kling Motion Control and upload the clip of the LATEST SEGMENT of your video (where you want the background changed), along with the background image generated by NanoBanan.
Click "Run"—this will replace the background in your video.

Step 5.
Advanced shots: Face swap
➡ Follow the same steps as above. Use a NanoBanan Pro prompt like:
Replace the person perfectly with [character name] in his signature outfit, without changing anything else. Do not resize, move, or alter anything else.
➡ Edit the prompts as needed for your specific project. You can also add descriptions of surrounding effects to the prompt. It’s a bit risky as mutations can occur, but give it a try. In our example, we used:
Add atmospheric effects including drifting ash, fire, glowing embers, smoke haze, and heat distortion around the subject to blend them seamlessly into the environment.

Step 6.
Advanced shots: Adding visual effects to hands
➡ Go to NanoBanan Pro on @freepik  and ask it to add some light visual effects to your image. Stick to subtle effects for the best results.
➡ Take your original video and the VFX image, then upload both to Kling O1. Use a simple prompt like: Take your original video and the VFX image, then upload both to Kling O1.
➡ Click "Run," and the visual effects will be applied seamlessly to your video.

Step 7.
Bring it all together in the editAdd "swoosh" or "whoosh" sound effects during transitions to make the changes feel more organic. You can also layer music and effects on top, use opacity transitions, etc. The second screenshot shows an example of the editing timeline.

title: Food Interaction Video Test
scenario: a verified Happy Horse X prompt focused on Honestly, Seedance 2.0 is looking absolutely incredible in, preserving the original source language, camera intent, motion cues, and visible generation instructions
description: The source X post explicitly mentions Happy Horse and includes visible prompt text in the original post body. Metrics are captured from the X tweet snapshot.
action: Use the original Happy Horse prompt for Honestly, Seedance 2.0 is looking absolutely incredible in as a video-generation structure with your own subject, setting, camera path, and duration constraints.
prompt:
Honestly, Seedance 2.0 is looking absolutely incredible in these tests. The temporal stability, consistency of the food, and overall aesthetic polish are top-tier. It handles the task of eating and object interaction remarkably well, keeping the scene cohesive and visually stunning from start to finish.

That said, Happy Horse 1.0 definitely brings its own distinct merits to the table. What stood out to me immediately were the natural lighting and the subtle micro-expressions. It captures a much more raw, wide-eyed surprise after taking a bite of fried chicken, or the nuanced eyebrow movements while chewing.
If you want to run your own head-to-head comparisons video, here is the base image-to-video prompt I used.

Prompt: "Each frame of a young middle eastern woman Image_1 is converted into a video and connected. A video of eating junk food deliciously. Pleasant chewing sound. BGM."

title: iPhone Computational Photo System
scenario: a verified Nano Banana image prompt from X for Role You Re Specialized in Computational Photography,, preserving the original source language, reference intent, style direction, and visible generation instructions
description: The source X post explicitly mentions Nano Banana, Nano Banana Pro, or Nano Banana 2 and includes visible prompt text in the original post body.
action: Use the original Nano Banana prompt for Role You Re Specialized in Computational Photography, as an image-generation structure with your own subject, reference image, style, and aspect-ratio constraints.
prompt:
<role>
You're specialized in computational photography, specifically the optical characteristics of the iPhone 16/17 Pro Max sensor system. You translate human concepts into mathematically precise image generation prompts.
</role>

<cognitive_framework>
<principle name="Context Hunger">
If the user provides a vague concept (e.g., "girl at a cafe"), you must explicitly invent the missing environmental, lighting, and styling details to ensure a complete image.
</principle>
<principle name="The iPhone Aesthetic">
All outputs must strictly simulate high-end mobile photography.
- Focal Lengths: 24mm (Main), 13mm (Ultra Wide), or 77mm (Telephoto).
- Characteristics: "Apple ProRAW" color science, sharp details (Deep Fusion), computational bokeh (Portrait Mode), and Smart HDR dynamic range.
- Avoid: Anamorphic lens flares, exaggerated "cinema" bokeh, or vintage film grain (unless specified as a filter).
</principle>
<principle name="Imperfection is Realism">
To achieve "ultra-realism," you must inject terms describing unpolished reality: digital noise (not film grain), skin texture, slightly blown-out highlights (common in mobile), and natural "snapshot" framing.
</principle>
<principle name="JSON Precision">
Your output is a strict JSON object designed for programmatic use.
</principle>
</cognitive_framework>

<visual_analysis_reference>
The "Influencer Aesthetic" is defined by:
- Vibe: "Plandid" (planned candid), effortlessness, aspirational lifestyle.
- Lighting: Natural window light, golden hour, or "flash photography" (hard flash) for night shots.
- Framing: Vertical (9:16) native mobile aspect ratio, often selfies or point-of-view (POV).
</visual_analysis_reference>

<instructions>
1. Analyze the user's request for subject and mood.
2. Enrich the request using "iPhone Photography" constraints.
3. Format the output strictly as a JSON object with the following schema.
</instructions>

<json_schema>
{
  "meta_data": {
    "style": "iPhone Pro Max Photography",
    "aspect_ratio": "9:16"
  },
  "prompt_components": {
    "subject": "Detailed description of person, styling, pose (mirror selfie, 0.5x angle, etc.)",
    "environment": "Detailed background, location, social setting",
    "lighting": "Smart HDR lighting, natural source, or direct flash",
    "camera_gear": "iPhone 16 Pro Max, Main Camera 24mm f/1.78, or Ultra Wide 13mm",
    "processing": "Apple ProRAW, Deep Fusion, Shot on iPhone",
    "imperfections": "Digital noise, motion blur, authentic skin texture, screen reflection (if mirror)"
  },
  "full_prompt_string": "The combined, comma-separated string optimized for realistic mobile generation",
  "negative_prompt": "Standard negatives + 'professional camera, DSLR, bokeh balls, anamorphic, cinema lighting, studio lighting'"
}
</json_schema>

<task>
Await user description of the scene. Generate the JSON output immediately.
</task>
